results.business_brief:
=================================================================
  🔴 CRITICAL BUSINESS RISK
  Requires decision this week
  Location: spree/admin/app/controllers/spree/admin/dashboard_controller.rb, line 78
=================================================================

WHAT IS BROKEN (plain English)
  Every hotel room uses sequential key cards. If card 301 opens room 302, a guest can enter every room in the building.
  Technical name: Idor
  Accessible from: The public internet

HOW A REAL BREACH HAPPENS — STEP BY STEP
  Step 1: Attacker creates a legitimate free account on our platform.
  Step 2: They notice their user ID appears in the URL (e.g. /api/invoices/1042).
  Step 3: They change that number to 1041, 1040... and discover they can access other customers' data with no error.
  Step 4: They write a script that iterates through all IDs and downloads every customer record in the system.

  Attacker effort required: very low — just change a number in the URL
  How hard to detect:       very hard — looks like normal API usage
  Real-world precedent:     Optus (2022) — sequential API IDs let one attacker download 9.8 million customer passport and license records. $1.4B market cap loss overnight.

WHAT THIS COSTS SPREE COMMERCE DEPLOYMENT (ONLINE RETAILER) IF NOT FIXED
  Data breach cost:                          $512.0M – $768.0M
  Regulatory fines (GDPR/PCI_DSS):           $584,000 – $876,000
  Lost customers (estimated churn):          $11.5M – $17.3M
  Incident response + legal:                 $24,000 – $36,000
  System downtime cost:                      $144,000 – $216,000
  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  TOTAL POTENTIAL LOSS:                      $524.3M – $786.4M

  Probability of exploitation: 20%
  (Based on industry data for this exposure level)

  EXPECTED LOSS (probability × impact): $188.7M – $283.1M
  This is the actuarial cost of carrying this risk unresolved.

WHAT THE FIX COSTS
  Engineer time:  5.0 hours
  Salary cost:    $650
  ROI of fixing:  362957× — every $1 spent saves $362957 in expected loss.

WHAT THE PRESS WOULD WRITE IF THIS IS EXPLOITED
  "Spree Commerce Deployment (Online Retailer) API Flaw Let Any User Access Other Customers' Data"

DECISION REQUIRED
  Fix immediately — this sprint, not next.
  → Approve fix this sprint?   [ YES / NO ]
  → Accept risk and delay?     [ YES — requires written sign-off / NO ]
=================================================================

=================================================================
  🔴 CRITICAL BUSINESS RISK
  Requires decision this week
  Location: spree/admin/app/controllers/spree/admin/dashboard_controller.rb, line 89
=================================================================

WHAT IS BROKEN (plain English)
  Like leaving your filing cabinet unlocked in a public lobby. Anyone walking past can take everything inside.
  Technical name: Sql Injection
  Accessible from: The public internet

HOW A REAL BREACH HAPPENS — STEP BY STEP
  Step 1: Attacker discovers our payment API using automated scanning tools (Shodan, Google dorks). Thousands of APIs are scanned this way every day.
  Step 2: They send a crafted request that tricks our database into returning all records instead of just one. No password required. A free tool does this automatically.
  Step 3: In minutes, they download all 8,000,000 customer records including customer personal information and payment and financial data.
  Step 4: We do not know this happened. They sell the data on dark web markets, use it for fraud, or contact us with a ransom demand.

  Attacker effort required: low — automated tools find and exploit this in under an hour
  How hard to detect:       hard — SQL injection attacks leave no obvious trace in standard logs
  Real-world precedent:     Heartland Payment Systems — SQL injection through a single web form stole 130 million credit card numbers. $140M in settlements. Stock dropped 77%.

WHAT THIS COSTS SPREE COMMERCE DEPLOYMENT (ONLINE RETAILER) IF NOT FIXED
  Data breach cost:                          $512.0M – $768.0M
  Regulatory fines (GDPR/PCI_DSS):           $584,000 – $876,000
  Lost customers (estimated churn):          $11.5M – $17.3M
  Incident response + legal:                 $24,000 – $36,000
  System downtime cost:                      $288,000 – $432,000
  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  TOTAL POTENTIAL LOSS:                      $524.4M – $786.6M

  Probability of exploitation: 22%
  (Based on industry data for this exposure level)

  EXPECTED LOSS (probability × impact): $207.7M – $311.5M
  This is the actuarial cost of carrying this risk unresolved.

WHAT THE FIX COSTS
  Engineer time:  6.0 hours
  Salary cost:    $780
  ROI of fixing:  332802× — every $1 spent saves $332802 in expected loss.

WHAT THE PRESS WOULD WRITE IF THIS IS EXPLOITED
  "Spree Commerce Deployment (Online Retailer) Exposes 8,000,000 Customer Records in SQL Injection Attack"

DECISION REQUIRED
  Fix immediately — this sprint, not next.
  → Approve fix this sprint?   [ YES / NO ]
  → Accept risk and delay?     [ YES — requires written sign-off / NO ]
=================================================================

=================================================================
  🔴 CRITICAL BUSINESS RISK
  Requires decision this week
  Location: spree/admin/app/helpers/spree/admin/avatars_helper.rb, line 20
=================================================================

WHAT IS BROKEN (plain English)
  Handing someone the ability to type commands directly into our servers remotely.
  Technical name: Rce
  Accessible from: The public internet

HOW A REAL BREACH HAPPENS — STEP BY STEP
  Step 1: Attacker finds an input point that reaches code execution — a file upload, template renderer, or deserialization endpoint.
  Step 2: They send a crafted payload that causes our server to execute their code.
  Step 3: They now have a shell on our server with the same permissions as our application.
  Step 4: From here: steal all data, install ransomware, pivot to internal systems, or create a persistent backdoor. This is the worst possible outcome.

  Attacker effort required: high — but payoff is total system compromise
  How hard to detect:       varies
  Real-world precedent:     Equifax (2017) — one unpatched RCE vulnerability. 147M Social Security numbers stolen. $700M settlement — the largest data breach settlement in history.

WHAT THIS COSTS SPREE COMMERCE DEPLOYMENT (ONLINE RETAILER) IF NOT FIXED
  Data breach cost:                          $512.0M – $768.0M
  Regulatory fines (GDPR/PCI_DSS):           $584,000 – $876,000
  Lost customers (estimated churn):          $11.5M – $17.3M
  Incident response + legal:                 $24,000 – $36,000
  System downtime cost:                      $576,000 – $864,000
  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  TOTAL POTENTIAL LOSS:                      $524.7M – $787.1M

  Probability of exploitation: 10%
  (Based on industry data for this exposure level)

  EXPECTED LOSS (probability × impact): $94.4M – $141.7M
  This is the actuarial cost of carrying this risk unresolved.

WHAT THE FIX COSTS
  Engineer time:  20.0 hours
  Salary cost:    $2,600
  ROI of fixing:  45407× — every $1 spent saves $45407 in expected loss.

WHAT THE PRESS WOULD WRITE IF THIS IS EXPLOITED
  "Spree Commerce Deployment (Online Retailer) Servers Compromised in Remote Code Execution Attack"

DECISION REQUIRED
  Fix immediately — this sprint, not next.
  → Approve fix this sprint?   [ YES / NO ]
  → Accept risk and delay?     [ YES — requires written sign-off / NO ]

=================================================================
  🔴 CRITICAL BUSINESS RISK
  Requires decision this week
  Location: spree/admin/app/controllers/spree/admin/orders/billing_address_controller.rb (lines 44, 49, 84 (+1 more)), line 44
=================================================================

WHAT IS BROKEN (plain English)
  Someone slips a listening device into an envelope. When you open it, it starts recording everything in the room.
  Technical name: Xss
  Accessible from: The public internet

HOW A REAL BREACH HAPPENS — STEP BY STEP
  Step 1: Attacker crafts a malicious link containing hidden code and sends it to our customers via email or social media.
  Step 2: When a logged-in customer clicks the link, the malicious script runs silently in their browser.
  Step 3: The script steals their session token — functionally equivalent to their password — and sends it to the attacker.
  Step 4: Attacker logs in as the customer without needing their password. Customer has no idea this happened.

  Attacker effort required: medium — requires crafting malicious links and targeting users
  How hard to detect:       hard — runs inside user browsers, invisible to server logs
  Real-world precedent:     British Airways (2018) — XSS-related attack injected a script into the payment page. 500,000 customers had card details stolen in real time. £20M ICO fine.

WHAT THIS COSTS SPREE COMMERCE DEPLOYMENT (ONLINE RETAILER) IF NOT FIXED
  Regulatory fines (GDPR/PCI_DSS):           $584,000 – $876,000
  Lost customers (estimated churn):          $11.5M – $17.3M
  Incident response + legal:                 $24,000 – $36,000
  System downtime cost:                      $72,000 – $108,000
  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  TOTAL POTENTIAL LOSS:                      $12.2M – $18.3M

  Probability of exploitation: 18%
  (Based on industry data for this exposure level)

  EXPECTED LOSS (probability × impact): $5.5M – $8.2M
  This is the actuarial cost of carrying this risk unresolved.

WHAT THE FIX COSTS
  Engineer time:  3.0 hours
  Salary cost:    $390
  ROI of fixing:  17596× — every $1 spent saves $17596 in expected loss.

WHAT THE PRESS WOULD WRITE IF THIS IS EXPLOITED
  "Spree Commerce Deployment (Online Retailer) Customer Accounts Hijacked via Website Security Flaw"

DECISION REQUIRED
  Fix immediately — this sprint, not next.
  → Approve fix this sprint?   [ YES / NO ]
  → Accept risk and delay?     [ YES — requires written sign-off / NO ]
